articles.headline: Chinese display manufacturing giant BOE makes foray into robotics, AI software
articles.summary: Apple supplier BOE Technology Group, the world's largest display maker, has established a new subsidiary to lead its foray into robotics and artificial intelligence software. Launched on Thursday, Beijing BOE Robot has a registered capital of 200 million yuan (US$28 million), according to corporate data provider Qichacha. The subsidiary was described as a developer of industrial robots and AI soft

articles.headline: Top-Performing ETF Areas of Q3
articles.summary: Small caps led Q3 gains, while other standout ETF areas are Bitcoin mining (WGMI, MNRS), cannabis (MSOS, WEED), Ethereum (ETH, ETHW) and rare earths (REMX).

articles.headline: Mixed Performance by the Yen Ahead of Inflation and the BoJ’s Meeting
articles.summary: After Japan reported a significantly smaller trade deficit than expected overnight GMT 16-17 September, the yen has gained overall against some currencies like the US dollar while losing ground against others like the euro and pound sterling.

articles.headline: Analysts Boost S&P 500 Target: ETFs in Focus
articles.summary: Many analysts are making bullish calls on the S&P 500 target due to earnings, AI and U.S. growth. This has put the focus on VOO, IVV, SPY and SPMO.

articles.headline: Rupee ends little changed, importer hedging caps lift from softer dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee closed nearly flat on Monday, as the boost from a broadly weaker dollar was blunted by persistent hedging demand from importers and lacklustre foreign portfolio flows.  While the currency rose to an intraday peak of 88.1475, traders pointed out that dollar demand from local importers, including oil companies, shaved its gains later in the session.  Data released on Monday showed t

articles.headline: Mexican Equities Offer the Possibility of Diversification
articles.summary: As U.S. economic expansion vastly outpaces growth in Mexico, do Mexican equities offer an opportunity for portfolio diversification?

articles.headline: 4 Factors That Could Boost S&P 500 Even Further in 2026
articles.summary: AI momentum, strong earnings, resilient U.S. economy, and likely Fed rate cuts could keep fueling the S&P 500 rally, benefiting ETFs like VOO, SPY and IVV.

articles.headline: 5 Best-Performing Sector ETFs of August
articles.summary: Marijuana, crypto, materials, healthcare and space ETFs led August gains despite tech volatility. These ETFs are MSOS, REMX, IHF, UFO, and ETHW.

articles.headline: ETFs Pull in $119.3B in August, Highest Monthly Total of 2025
articles.summary: Vanguard’s VOO and iShares’ IVV topped the August inflows list.

articles.headline: Asian Value Stock Picks For Potential Capital Growth
articles.summary: As Asian markets continue to navigate a complex landscape of economic indicators and geopolitical tensions, investors are increasingly focused on identifying opportunities for potential capital growth. In this environment, stocks that are perceived as undervalued may offer attractive prospects for those seeking to capitalize on market inefficiencies and long-term value creation.

articles.headline: China's GCL expects more information soon on polysilicon restructuring
articles.summary: BEIJING (Reuters) -A GCL Technology Holdings official signalled in a call with investors on Sunday that more information on a plan to restructure China's polysilicon industry would be released soon.  "We believe that clearer information should come out soon about the reform, and we will have a better grasp of how our future cash flow will go," GCL's Chief Financial Officer Yang Wenzhong said in re
